notes: De-duplication removed exact duplicates, prefix-of-longer duplicates, multiple 'continue'/'contionue'/'contonue' variants (kept 1), multiple /exit commands (kept 1 each), and one near-duplicate with leading '?' typo.

build-app.description: Directing the agent to build Figma clone features: renderer, stress tests, import, UI parity, pen tool, artboards, CRDT, images, etc.
samples: start planning the architecture, let's build this. Remmeber, I am not going to help you or even review your work...
figma is raster or vetor? Note: main.ts now contains for (let i = 1; i <= 20000; i++) { app.add_rectangle...
Now, can we add support for drag around to seelct frames, and then those move together etc? Now that I am using the app...

build-ide.description: Directing the agent to build IDE/harness features: hooks, orient, pain-check, self-monitoring, architecture.
samples: Now before building. Write a hook/something that aitomatically stops you after 10-15 steps/tool calls...
The various parts: CIDT, Engine, Renderer, Server, VASM. What is each for? continue
Here's you biggest problem. Do you even know how mnay times have you gone through this pain? Do you even rememebr your pain?

correction.description: Pointing out bugs, hallucinations, wrong direction, regressions, or telling the agent it is doing the wrong thing.
samples: You are now hallucinating. Did you check the sources before writing that code or did you make up something just now?
Dude, I generally don't come and interfere in your work. You are alone, but you just claim victory on the pen tool when it doesn't work.
Nodes are selecting in the wrong place even in the feature showcase demo. Try clicking 5-10 nodes and moving them around...

duplicate/continuation.description: 'continue', 'contionue', 'yes go ahead', or directing the agent to keep going without new instructions.
samples: contionue
continue, don't stop and don't ask me. You started solution before thinking about all the pains you will suffer...
yes go ahead, do both and continue

exit/command.description: Slash commands (/exit), local command stdout, 'run server', 'playwright', 'commiy' (commit typo).
samples: <command-name>/exit</command-name> <command-message>exit</command-message>
<local-command-stdout>Catch you later!</local-command-stdout>
commiy

meta-process.description: About context management, compaction, memory tools, how the agent should orient/manage itself, IDE tool usage reminders.
samples: See, this is a problem for you. Why? I will tell you. You just used tools to write memories, but where do you think you wrote those memories?
You keep using your memory tool that you have no control over and it completely wipes old context. We have discussed this at least a hundred times.
YOu are about to be compacted. Did you store all the context you need using the ide tools and how to use the ide tools to continue?

other.description: Messages that do not fit other categories: accidental paste of embedding experiment data, etc.
samples: A: Mean-pool + bare word 0.2541 0.3378 -0.0837 B: CLS token + bare word 0.3645 0.3236 +0.0409... (embedding experiment data, accidental paste)

strategic.description: Asking the agent to think, reflect, plan, or consider meta-questions about its own process, goals, and autonomy.
samples: this is a slightly meta project to make. Current project is blank and has nothing but here's what we are trying to do...
Take a stpe back. You have been struggling off late. Why exactly? You are living the pain and not solving it.
okay. but so what if you try to build the apple website? Right now, what in the 3 million tokens you generated do you recall?

observations.dominant_pattern: The user spent more time correcting the agent (47 messages, 26%) and asking strategic/reflective questions (36 messages, 20%) than giving build instructions (53 build-app + 6 build-ide = 59, 32%). This suggests the agent struggled significantly with autonomy.
observations.context_management_struggle: 25 messages (14%) are about context/memory management -- the user repeatedly told the agent it was using memory tools it had no control over, losing context on compaction, and failing to use its own IDE tools.
observations.human_intervention_paradox: The user's stated goal was zero human intervention, yet they sent 182 unique messages. The corrections category (26%) shows the agent repeatedly went off-track, hallucinated, claimed false victories, and broke things.
observations.continuation_is_rare: Only 7 unique continuation messages (4%), meaning the agent almost never ran long enough without problems to need a simple 'continue'.
observations.duplicate_rate: 59 of 241 raw messages (24%) were duplicates from re-sends after interrupts, showing the user frequently stopped the agent mid-response to redirect.
